# SimpleSign — Complete API Reference for LLMs

> Pure .NET library for PAdES (PDF Advanced Electronic Signatures). Sign, validate, inspect PDF documents. ETSI EN 319 142 compliant. No third-party crypto. Native AOT compatible. .NET 8 / .NET 10.

## Installation

```bash
# Most common — full PAdES signing & validation
dotnet add package SimpleSign.PAdES

# Brazilian PKI (ICP-Brasil, Gov.br, Lei 14.063)
dotnet add package SimpleSign.Brasil

# HTML to PDF conversion (independent, no signing)
dotnet add package SimpleSign.HtmlToPdf

# Everything
dotnet add package SimpleSign
```

## Namespaces

```csharp
using SimpleSign.PAdES;              // SimpleSigner, DeferredSigner, BatchSigner
using SimpleSign.PAdES.Validation;   // PdfSignatureValidator, ValidationOptions
using SimpleSign.PAdES.Inspection;   // PdfSignatureInspector
using SimpleSign.Core.Crypto;        // TsaPool, HashAlgorithmName
using SimpleSign.Brasil;             // IcpBrasilValidator, GovBrValidator
using SimpleSign.Brasil.Extensions;  // IcpBrasilValidationResult extensions
```

---

## Signing API

### Simple Sign (one-shot)

```csharp
using SimpleSign.PAdES;
using System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates;

var cert = X509CertificateLoader.LoadPkcs12FromFile("cert.pfx", "password");
var pdf = File.ReadAllBytes("document.pdf");

byte[] signedPdf = await SimpleSigner
    .Document(pdf)
    .WithCertificate(cert)
    .SignAsync();

File.WriteAllBytes("signed.pdf", signedPdf);
```

### Full Builder Options

```csharp
byte[] signedPdf = await SimpleSigner
    .Document(pdf)
    .WithCertificate(cert)
    // Timestamp authority
    .WithTimestamp("http://timestamp.digicert.com")
    // Long-term validation (embeds OCSP + CRL)
    .WithLtv()
    // Visible signature appearance
    .WithAppearance(appearance => appearance
        .OnPage(1)
        .AtPosition(100, 100, 300, 150)   // x, y, width, height
        .WithText("Signed by {signer}\n{date}")
        .WithImage(logoBytes)
        .WithQrCode("https://verify.example.com/{id}"))
    // Metadata
    .WithReason("Contract approval")
    .WithLocation("São Paulo, Brazil")
    .WithSignerName("Jane Doe")
    .WithContactInfo("jane@example.com")
    // Hash algorithm (default: SHA-256)
    .WithHashAlgorithm(HashAlgorithmName.SHA384)
    // Custom HTTP client for TSA/OCSP/CRL
    .WithHttpClient(httpClient)
    // Logging
    .WithLogger(logger)
    .SignAsync();
```

### Deferred Signing (Two-Phase)

Use when the private key is on a client device (web apps, mobile, HSM):

```csharp
using SimpleSign.PAdES;

// Phase 1: Server prepares the hash
var prepared = await DeferredSigner.PrepareAsync(pdfBytes, cert);
byte[] hashToSign = prepared.HashToSign;  // Send to client

// Phase 2: Client signs with private key (any algorithm: RSA, ECDSA, etc.)
byte[] clientSignature = SignWithPrivateKey(hashToSign);

// Phase 3: Server embeds the signature
byte[] signedPdf = await DeferredSigner.CompleteAsync(prepared.SessionData, clientSignature);
```

#### Deferred Builder Pattern

```csharp
var builder = new DeferredSignerBuilder(pdfBytes, cert)
    .WithSignerName("Jane Doe")
    .WithReason("Contract approval")
    .WithTimestamp("http://timestamp.digicert.com")
    .WithLtv()
    .WithAppearance(a => a.OnPage(1).AtPosition(50, 50, 200, 80));

var prepared = await builder.PrepareAsync();
byte[] signature = await clientDevice.SignAsync(prepared.HashToSign);
byte[] signedPdf = await builder.CompleteAsync(prepared.SessionData, signature);
```

### Batch Signing

Sign multiple documents with shared resources (connection pool, cert):

```csharp
using SimpleSign.PAdES;

var batch = BatchSigner.Create(cert)
    .WithTimestamp("http://timestamp.digicert.com")
    .WithLtv()
    .WithConcurrency(8)
    .Build();

var documents = new[] { pdf1, pdf2, pdf3 };
var results = await batch.SignAsync(documents);

Console.WriteLine($"Succeeded: {results.Succeeded}");
Console.WriteLine($"Failed: {results.Failed}");
Console.WriteLine($"Time: {results.ElapsedMs}ms");
```

### TSA Connection Pool

Resilient timestamp authority with automatic failover:

```csharp
using SimpleSign.Core.Crypto;

var pool = new TsaPool([
    "http://timestamp.digicert.com",
    "http://tsa.starfieldtech.com",
    "http://timestamp.sectigo.com"
]);

// Use with signer
var signed = await SimpleSigner.Document(pdf)
    .WithCertificate(cert)
    .WithTimestampPool(pool)
    .SignAsync();
```

---

## Validation API

### Validate Signatures

```csharp
using SimpleSign.PAdES.Validation;

var validator = new PdfSignatureValidator(new ValidationOptions
{
    CheckRevocation = true,          // OCSP + CRL
    TrustSystemRoots = true,         // Use OS certificate store
    NetworkTimeout = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(15),
});

var results = await validator.ValidateAsync(File.OpenRead("signed.pdf"));

foreach (var r in results)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"Field: {r.FieldName}");
    Console.WriteLine($"  Valid: {r.IsValid}");
    Console.WriteLine($"  Integrity: {r.IsIntegrityValid}");
    Console.WriteLine($"  Chain: {r.IsCertificateChainValid}");
    Console.WriteLine($"  Timestamp: {r.HasValidTimestamp}");
    Console.WriteLine($"  Signer: {r.SignerName}");
    Console.WriteLine($"  Time: {r.SigningTime}");
    Console.WriteLine($"  Algorithm: {r.DigestAlgorithmName}");
}
```

### Custom Trust Anchors

```csharp
var validator = new PdfSignatureValidator(new ValidationOptions
{
    TrustedRoots = new[] { rootCaCert, intermediateCaCert },
    CheckRevocation = true,
});
```

---

## Inspection API

Fast metadata extraction without cryptographic validation:

```csharp
using SimpleSign.PAdES.Inspection;

var result = await PdfSignatureInspector.InspectAsync(File.OpenRead("doc.pdf"));

Console.WriteLine($"Signatures: {result.Signatures.Count}");
foreach (var sig in result.Signatures)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"  {sig.FieldName}: {sig.SignerName}");
    Console.WriteLine($"    Time: {sig.SigningTime}");
    Console.WriteLine($"    Algorithm: {sig.DigestAlgorithm}");
    Console.WriteLine($"    Has Timestamp: {sig.HasTimestamp}");
    Console.WriteLine($"    Certificate: {sig.Certificate.Subject}");
}
```

---

## Brazilian PKI (ICP-Brasil)

### Chain Validation with ICP-Brasil

```csharp
using SimpleSign.Brasil;

var validator = new IcpBrasilValidator();
var results = await validator.ValidateAsync(File.OpenRead("signed.pdf"));

foreach (var r in results)
{
    if (r is IcpBrasilValidationResult icpResult)
    {
        Console.WriteLine($"CPF: {icpResult.Cpf}");
        Console.WriteLine($"CNPJ: {icpResult.Cnpj}");
        Console.WriteLine($"Policy: {icpResult.PolicyName}");
    }
}
```

### CPF/CNPJ Extraction

```csharp
using SimpleSign.Brasil.Extensions;

var result = await validator.ValidateAsync(stream);
var icpResult = result.First() as IcpBrasilValidationResult;
string cpf = icpResult.CpfFormatted;    // "123.456.789-00"
string cnpj = icpResult.CnpjFormatted;  // "12.345.678/0001-00"
```

### Health Professional Data (e-Prescriptions)

```csharp
var healthData = icpResult.HealthProfessional;
Console.WriteLine($"CRM: {healthData.RegistrationNumber}");
Console.WriteLine($"Council: {healthData.ProfessionalCouncil}");
Console.WriteLine($"State: {healthData.State}");
```

### VALIDAR ITI Portal Link

Generate verification URLs for QR code embedding:

```csharp
using SimpleSign.Brasil;

string validarUrl = ValidarItiUrlBuilder.Build(signedPdfBytes);
// https://validar.iti.gov.br/validar?...
```

---

## Extension Points

| Extension | Interface / Pattern |
|---|---|
| Custom trust anchors | `ITrustAnchorProvider` |
| Custom hash algorithm | `HashAlgorithmName` parameter |
| External signer (HSM/KMS) | `Func<byte[], Task<byte[]>>` callback |
| Custom HTTP | `IHttpClientProvider` / `HttpClient` injection |
| Custom logging | `ILogger<T>` injection |
| Country extensions | `ICountryExtension` |
| Chain validation | `IChainValidationProvider` |
| Certificate caching | `ICertificateCache` |

---

## HTML to PDF

Independent module — no signing dependency:

```csharp
using SimpleSign.HtmlToPdf;

byte[] pdf = HtmlToPdfConverter.Html(htmlString)
    .WithPageSize(PageSize.A4)
    .WithMargins(top: 40, right: 40, bottom: 40, left: 40)
    .Convert();

File.WriteAllBytes("output.pdf", pdf);
```

---

## Error Handling

```csharp
try
{
    var signed = await SimpleSigner.Document(pdf)
        .WithCertificate(cert)
        .SignAsync();
}
catch (SigningException ex) when (ex.InnerException is TimeoutException)
{
    // TSA timeout — retry or use pool
}
catch (SigningException ex)
{
    // General signing failure
    Console.WriteLine(ex.Message);
}
catch (InvalidPdfException ex)
{
    // PDF is malformed or encrypted
}
```

---

## Common Integration Patterns

### ASP.NET Core Minimal API

```csharp
app.MapPost("/sign", async (IFormFile file, [FromServices] X509Certificate2 cert) =>
{
    using var ms = new MemoryStream();
    await file.CopyToAsync(ms);

    var signed = await SimpleSigner.Document(ms.ToArray())
        .WithCertificate(cert)
        .WithTimestamp("http://timestamp.digicert.com")
        .WithLtv()
        .SignAsync();

    return Results.File(signed, "application/pdf", "signed.pdf");
});
```

### Dependency Injection

```csharp
// Register certificate from configuration
builder.Services.AddSingleton(sp =>
{
    var config = sp.GetRequiredService<IConfiguration>();
    return X509CertificateLoader.LoadPkcs12FromFile(config["Cert:Path"]!, config["Cert:Password"]!);
});

// Register validator
builder.Services.AddSingleton(new PdfSignatureValidator(new ValidationOptions
{
    CheckRevocation = true,
    TrustSystemRoots = true,
}));
```

### Azure Key Vault / HSM Integration

```csharp
// Use external signer callback for HSM-based keys
var signed = await SimpleSigner.Document(pdf)
    .WithCertificate(publicCert)  // Public cert only (no private key)
    .WithExternalSigner(async hash =>
    {
        // Sign hash using Azure Key Vault, AWS KMS, Google Cloud KMS, etc.
        return await keyVaultClient.SignAsync(keyId, SignatureAlgorithm.RS256, hash);
    })
    .WithTimestamp("http://timestamp.digicert.com")
    .SignAsync();
```

---

## Requirements

- .NET 8 or .NET 10 (multi-target)
- No native/COM dependencies
- No third-party cryptography
- Runs on Windows, macOS, Linux
- Native AOT compatible
- MIT licensed
